channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: From physics-based intelligence to molecular design: linking mechanistic learning across scales
item.description: This article explores the integration of physics-based computational approaches with artificial intelligence to advance molecular design by establishing mechanistic learning frameworks across multiple biological and chemical scales. The work, authored by researchers at Shanghai Jiao Tong University

item.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
item.description: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

item.title: Profiles of medication literacy and associated factors among young and middle-aged hypertensive patients in China: A latent profile analysis
item.description: This study uses latent profile analysis to identify distinct patterns of medication literacy among young and middle-aged hypertensive patients in China and examines the factors associated with these profiles. Understanding medication literacy patterns in this population may inform targeted intervent

item.title: Human genetics across levels of biological organization
item.description: Genetic variation influences biological processes across scales; however, deciphering how effects propagate from molecules to whole organisms remains a key challenge in human genetics. In this Review, Laman Trip and Beltrao discuss how to link genetic variati…

item.title: China has cracked down on AI companions. what can we learn from this?
item.description: Chatbots that relieve loneliness have a major downside, and we must safeguard against it.

item.title: Medidata launches medidata plus, an ai-native foundation built to scale clinical portfolios
item.description: Built on an award-winning AI foundation, Medidata Plus removes traditional barriers, empowering sponsors and CROs to scale intelligence across entire portfolios NEW YORK, July 23, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Medidata, a Dassault Systèmes brand and leading provide…

item.title: Get the nitty-gritty right: the impact of rbi's data governance proposals will depend on finer details
item.description: The central bank’s Guidance on Regulatory Expectations for Data Governance is timely, given the need for greater discipline in data usage by banks and NBFCs. Public consultations would be needed to get the details right—and some trade-offs will need to be mad…

item.title: How rwanda is using data to deliver better health care
item.description: By connecting health data across the system, Rwanda is enabling faster decisions, shorter waits and better care.

item.title: Senior white house official claims china’s k3 model stolen from anthropic
item.description: And accuses Thailand hosting hardware that may have helped distillation attack

item.title: Immunostimulatory lipogel implant enhances cancer immunotherapy
item.description: Researchers have developed an immunostimulatory lipogel implant designed to enhance the efficacy of cancer immunotherapy. This localized implant approach represents a novel delivery strategy for improving anti-tumor immune responses. The work was published in Nature Nanotechnology and involved colla

item.title: Oncology drug development in the UK: analysis of the past 25 years and focus on the 2026 reboot of the clinical trial regulatory framework
item.description: This analysis examines oncology drug development in the United Kingdom over the past 25 years, with particular emphasis on the anticipated 2026 regulatory framework changes for clinical trials. The study provides insights into historical trends in UK oncology drug development and evaluates how the f

item.title: STAT+: European regulators take steps to bolster clinical trial transparency
item.description: The European Medicines Agency is taking what advocacy groups are calling unexpectedly “rapid action” to ensure that clinical trial results are made public as required by law.

item.title: A father’s mission to save his daughter, and allegations on telehealth GLP-1 prescribing
item.description: Has a wanted fugitive been posing as a biotech executive? And what does it take to develop a therapy for an ultra-rare disease? Find out on this week's episode of…

item.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
item.description: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

item.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
item.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

item.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
item.description: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

item.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: Mapping drivers of life expectancy change in asia from 1990 to 2023
item.description: Using the Global Burden of Disease Study data across Asian countries and territories, life expectancy trends and the effects of cause-specific mortality and risk factors on changes in these trends are analysed.

item.title: Precise dna base editing using alphafold3-based contact modelling
item.description: ContactSeek is an AlphaFold3-driven model that can improve the precision of genome-editing tools.

item.title: The anthropic-physical intelligence rumor roiling AI twitter | techcrunch
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: Openai admits it was the source of the agent swarm that attacked hugging face
item.description: Sandboxed experiment found itself a zero day, escaped onto the open internet and stole data

item.title: Dimension capital's $800m third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming | techcrunch
item.description: Dimension Capital's $800M third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: FDA Announces First Participant Selected for TEMPO for Digital Health Devices Pilot
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced the first manufacturer selected for the Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot.

item.title: FDA Takes Further Steps to Remove Outdated Authorizations for Color Additives in Food
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced two actions targeting petroleum-based color additives in food, continuing the agency’s broader efforts to Make America Healthy Again.

item.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
item.description: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

item.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
item.description: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

item.title: In silico identification and biophysical characterization of candidate antimicrobial peptides from the indian marine microbiome targeting multidrug-resistant eskape pathogens
item.description: The global health crisis of antimicrobial resistance necessitates the discovery of new antibacterial agents. Underexplored marine microbiomes, particularly from the biodiverse Indian coast, represent a rich potential source of antimicrobial peptides (AMPs). T…

item.title: Large vision model framework for automated c. elegans analysis: from static morphometry to dynamic neural activity
item.description: Author summary The small roundworm Caenorhabditis elegans is widely used by scientists to study fundamental biological questions, such as aging and how the brain works. A crucial part of this research involves analyzing images and videos to measure the worm’s…

item.title: Daily briefing: can the world finally eradicate polio?
item.description: Some researchers think the current eradication strategy is outdated. Plus, goats hurt their brains when they butt heads and what scientist will never hand over to AI.

item.title: Biotalent canada and bioscience association manitoba launch joint AI training initiative
item.description: Collaboration expands access to AI Fundamentals for the Bio-economy, helping Manitoba organizations build confidence in using AI responsibly WINNIPEG, Manitoba — BioTalent Canada and Bioscience Association Manitoba (BAM) today announced a collaboration to exp…

item.title: Openai’s rogue hacking incident was a warning shot. will it be a wake-up call to finally create AI safety regulation?
item.description: AI policy experts and safety researchers call the AI agents' attack on Hugging Face "a wake up call."

item.title: Calls for guardrails grow as openai discloses 'unprecedented' autonomous cyber attack
item.description: In a striking incident that highlights the growing risks of unchecked advanced artificial intelligence development, ChatGPT maker OpenAI admitted Tuesday that one of its AI models autonomously breached the systems of the prominent open-source platform Hugging…

item.title: Fox entertainment exec says AI should ‘support human creativity’ as studio joins ariam content coalition
item.description: Fox Entertainment has joined Disney, the New York Times, Adobe and other members of the Alliance for Responsible Innovation in the Arts & Media (ARIAM), becoming the latest entertainment company to advocate for AI guardrails as many figure out how to navigate…

item.title: What marty makary actually got done as FDA commissioner
item.description: Marty Makary resigned as FDA commissioner amid controversy. But his reforms to drug review times and AI are the agency's biggest modernization in decades.

item.title: Bostongene and kyoto university establish strategic partnership to advance biomarker discovery for combination immunotherapy
item.description: TOKYO & WALTHAM, Mass.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Jul 22, 2026-- BostonGene, the developer of the leading AI model for tumor and immune biology, today announced a strategic research partnership with Kyoto University, a research institution known for its groundbreaking…

item.title: The future of health optimization through genetic insights
item.description: Health optimization is no longer limited to following general wellness advice or standard treatment plans. Advances in genetics, biotechnology, and data-driven healthcare are making it possible to understand the unique biological characteristics of each indiv…

item.title: Onc names 9 ehignite challenge phase one winners
item.description: Nearly 100 submissions were narrowed to nine winning teams who sought to turn electronic health information exports into actionable insights for patients and clinicians.

item.title: AI driving increased use of real-time data by accountants enabling better business decisions, but integrity concerns widespread
item.description: CFOs and finance teams are harnessing an explosion in data and AI technology to drive better business insights. But a new survey shows most have concerns over the integrity of AI-generated analysis A major report, Enabling finance insight, from two of the wor…

item.title: Aws lambda durable functions now supports customer managed key encryption
item.description: AWS Lambda durable functions now supports encryption of durable execution data with an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Lambda durable functions lets you build long-running, reliable workflows directly in your Lambda function code wi…

item.title: FDA announces first participant selected for tempo for digital health devices pilot
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced the first manufacturer selected for the Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot.

item.title: Fmrp-lean: a hipaa-compliant ai-augmented lims architecture for end-to-end clinical assay workflow optimization
item.description: Clinical biomarker workflows in translational research settings often rely on spreadsheet-driven tracking, manual quality control (QC) reconciliation, and loosely integrated systems, resulting in limited state visibility, delayed reporting, and increased oper…

item.title: Association of triglyceride-glucose index (tyg) and a body shape index (absi) with cognitive decline and dementia risk
item.description: Background Longitudinal evidence linking the body shape index (ABSI) to dementia and its subtypes remains scarce. Furthermore, the use of ABSI and TyG-ABSI as predictors of cognitive decline and dementia is infrequent. Therefore, this study aimed to examine t…

item.title: Low-latency stage-adaptive cascade architecture for real time non-stationary noise filtering
item.description: In many real-time measurement and monitoring systems, the quality of acquired signals is often severely degraded by complex environmental noise sources with non-stationary properties, rendering analysis, important feature extraction, and decision-making unrel…

item.title: Age at first marriage among women in lesotho: multilevel survival analysis- insight from 2023-24 demographic and health survey data
item.description: Background Early marriage remains a significant public health and social issue in Lesotho, with profound implications for women's health, education, and economic empowerment. Despite legal frameworks aimed at curbing the practice, a substantial proportion of …

item.title: Athennian and pwc UK collaborate to bring ai-powered intelligence to global subsidiary governance
item.description: The collaboration brings together Athennian’s governance operations platform and PwC’s deep expertise in global subsidiary governance to deliver AI-powered intelligence that provides real-time visibility into legal entities, governance obligations and complia…

item.title: Game on: what innovation minister, ude, plans to do @ enugu games conference
item.description: By Prince Osuagwu It will be game -on, in the morning of July, 30, 2026, when the Minister of Innovation, Science and Technology, Dr. Kingsley Udeh, mounts the podium at the International Conference Centre, ICC, Enugu, to discuss the innovations technology ha…

item.title: Openai scored an own goal with huggingface attack, showing how open chinese models are winning
item.description: Closed models with guardrails can still cause harm, but may also not be able to fix problems they caused

item.title: Openai scored an own goal with hugging face attack, showing how open chinese models are winning
item.description: Closed models with guardrails can still cause harm, but may also not be able to fix problems they caused

item.title: Google cloud is killing it
item.description: It's Alphabet's fastest-growing business and now makes up more than a fifth of the juggernaut's revenue and operating profit

item.title: The white house is trying to figure out what to do about chinese AI
item.description: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.
